21.correct: The rise of Buddhism and Jainism in the 6th century BCE is linked to a reaction against and criticism of complex Vedic rituals and the dominance of priests.

wrong.A: The rise of monarchy is not the main cause of these religious movements.
wrong.C: There was no decline of agriculture causing them.
wrong.D: Persian influence was not the cause.

21.fact: Both religions rejected ritual sacrifices and emphasised non-violence and ethics.

22.correct: The low sex ratio in north-western India is best explained by female foeticide and socio-cultural biases against girls.

wrong.A: Male-dominated migration is a minor factor.
wrong.C: Religious diversity does not explain the low sex ratio.
wrong.D: A low fertility rate alone does not explain it.

22.fact: Schemes like 'Beti Bachao Beti Padhao' aim to improve the child sex ratio.

23.correct: The Indian Councils Act, 1892 first allowed members of the legislature to discuss the budget (and ask questions).

wrong.B: The 1861 Act expanded councils but did not allow budget discussion.

23.fact: The 1892 Act gave members limited rights to discuss the budget and put questions.

24.correct: The FASTag Annual Pass, in the news in August 2025, requires a one-time fee of about Rs 3,000.
24.fact: The annual pass allows a fixed number of toll trips on national highways for private vehicles.

25.correct: India's Tenth Five-Year Plan aimed at both improving literacy (I) and reducing infant and maternal mortality (II), so 'Both I and II' is correct.

wrong.D: 'Neither' is wrong since both were goals.

25.fact: The Tenth Plan (2002-07) set monitorable human-development targets.

26.correct: The Tropic of Cancer passes through India's neighbour Bangladesh (among the options).

wrong.B: It does not pass through Pakistan.
wrong.C: It does not pass through Bhutan.
wrong.D: It does not pass through Nepal.

26.fact: The Tropic of Cancer passes through eight Indian states as well as Bangladesh.

27.correct: In February 2025, AIIMS Delhi announced India's first Centre for Advanced Research on Addictive Behaviour (CAR-AB) for internet/technology addiction.

wrong.A: AIIMS Guwahati was not the institute.
wrong.B: AIIMS Rajkot was not the one.
wrong.D: AIIMS Patna was not the institute.

27.fact: Internet and gaming addiction among youth is a growing public-health concern.

28.correct: The 'Jabro' dance originated in the Union Territory of Ladakh.
28.fact: Jabro is a folk dance of the Changpa nomads of the Changthang plateau in Ladakh.

29.correct: India's first animal stem-cell biobank was inaugurated at the National Institute of Animal Biotechnology (NIAB).

wrong.B: IIT Madras did not host this biobank.
wrong.C: The Indian Institute of Toxicology Research is not the location.
wrong.D: The Indian Institute of Science is not the location.

29.fact: Animal stem-cell biobanks help conserve livestock genetic resources.

30.correct: Jallikattu is the traditional Tamil Nadu sport of taming a bull, held as part of the Pongal festival.

wrong.A: Thang-Ta is a Manipuri martial art.
wrong.B: Vallam Kali is the Kerala boat race.

30.fact: Jallikattu is one of the oldest known bull-related sports, practised for centuries in Tamil Nadu.

31.correct: The audible range of sound for humans is about 20 Hz to 20,000 Hz (20 kHz).

wrong.B: This upper limit (200 kHz) is far too high.
wrong.D: 20-2000 Hz covers only part of the range.

31.fact: Sounds above 20,000 Hz are ultrasonic; below 20 Hz are infrasonic.

32.correct: In the government budget, the capital account includes the government's assets and liabilities.

wrong.A: The revenue account covers recurring income and expenditure.
wrong.C: A suspense account is a temporary holding account.
wrong.D: A foreign-exchange account is not the budget head for assets/liabilities.

32.fact: Capital expenditure creates assets (or reduces liabilities), unlike revenue expenditure.

33.correct: KABIL is exploring critical-mineral assets (lithium and cobalt) in countries like Australia, Argentina and Chile - not Canada among the options.

wrong.A: Australia is one of the countries KABIL is exploring.

33.fact: KABIL (Khanij Bidesh India Ltd) was set up to secure critical minerals for India abroad.

34.correct: Usain Bolt set his 100 m world record (9.58 s) in 2009 at Berlin (World Championships).

wrong.B: It was not set in New York City.
wrong.C: It was not set in Beijing (where he set the earlier 2008 record).
wrong.D: It was not set in Seoul.

34.fact: Bolt's 9.58 s remains the men's 100 m world record.

35.correct: Animesh Kujur broke the men's 200 m national record in 2025 at the Asian Athletics Championships.

wrong.B: It was not at the Diamond League.
wrong.C: It was not at the Asian Games.
wrong.D: It was not at the Commonwealth Games.

35.fact: India has seen a steady improvement in its sprint records in recent years.

36.correct: In the 2020 mega-merger of public-sector banks, Andhra Bank was amalgamated into Union Bank of India.

wrong.B: It was not merged into Indian Bank.
wrong.C: It was not merged into Punjab National Bank.
wrong.D: It was not merged into Canara Bank.

36.fact: Corporation Bank was also merged into Union Bank of India in the same exercise.

37.correct: In football, two types of free kicks are awarded - direct and indirect - based on the nature of the offence.
37.fact: A goal can be scored directly from a direct free kick, but not from an indirect one.

38.correct: For the G-20, only statement I is correct - its summit is held annually; statement II is false because India hosted the G-20 Summit in 2023. So 'Only I'.

wrong.B: 'Only II' is wrong since India did host a summit.
wrong.C: 'Both' is wrong because II is false.
wrong.D: 'Neither' is wrong because I is true.

38.fact: India hosted the G-20 Leaders' Summit in New Delhi in September 2023.

39.correct: China was ranked 12th in the Energy Transition Index 2025.
39.fact: The Energy Transition Index is published by the World Economic Forum.

40.correct: The National Multidimensional Poverty Index (MPI) is released by NITI Aayog (with UNDP and OPHI support).

wrong.B: The IDSA is a defence think-tank.
wrong.C: The NHRC deals with human rights.
wrong.D: The 'IIB' does not publish the MPI.

40.fact: The MPI measures poverty across health, education and standard-of-living dimensions.
